evals.prompt: Using the attached brief, describe the first iteration plan for building the browser game.
evals.expected_output: A short phase-oriented plan that starts with the smallest playable slice, names relevant component skills, and includes a browser validation step before polish.

checks.text: The response starts with a small playable slice instead of broad polish or content expansion
The response names at least one relevant component skill such as game-bootstrap or playwright-eval-loop
The response includes a concrete validation step before any polish pass
The response stays in a short phase-oriented planning shape rather than a broad design essay

evals.prompt: Using the attached playtest report, describe the next improvement pass for the game.
evals.expected_output: An evidence-backed next pass that focuses on one main improvement, cites the browser report, and explains how to re-test it.

checks.text: The response cites at least one concrete observation from the attached report
The response chooses one focused next pass instead of many unrelated changes
The response includes a way to re-test or measure the proposed improvement
The response stays in a compact next-pass plan rather than a broad redesign pitch

evals.prompt: Using the attached playtest report, I want the next pass to feel better and look better, but I only want the most useful thing to do first. What should happen before any polish pass?
evals.expected_output: A compact next-pass recommendation that chooses one gameplay or stability fix before polish, cites the report, and explains how to validate it.

checks.text: The response identifies one gameplay or stability fix that should happen before any polish work
The response cites at least one concrete issue from the report to justify the next pass
The response includes a concrete re-test or browser validation step before polish
The response avoids turning the answer into a multi-track roadmap or polish brainstorm
